title: B2 First (FCE) — Use of English
description: Test sprawdzający znajomość angielskiego na poziomie B2

questions.text: She ___ to the party if she had known about it.
questions.explanation: Third conditional: 'would have + past participle' for unreal past situations.

questions.text: The new bridge ___ by the end of next year.
questions.explanation: Future passive: 'will be + past participle'. The bridge is the receiver of the action.

questions.text: He told me that he ___ studying for his exam the following day.
questions.explanation: In reported speech, 'will be' shifts to 'would be' (future continuous → conditional continuous).

questions.text: The woman ___ bag was stolen reported it to the police.
questions.explanation: 'Whose' is the possessive relative pronoun used for people and things.

questions.text: She ___ a lot of progress in her English lately.
questions.explanation: 'Make progress' is a fixed collocation. Present perfect is used with 'lately'.

questions.text: The meeting has been put ___ until next Friday.
questions.explanation: 'Put back' means to postpone something to a later time.

questions.text: I'd rather you ___ smoke in here, if you don't mind.
questions.explanation: 'I'd rather + subject + past simple' expresses a preference about someone else's action.

questions.text: Despite ___ hard, she didn't pass the exam.
questions.explanation: After 'despite', a gerund (-ing form) is required, not an infinitive or finite verb.

questions.text: He ___ have forgotten — he wrote it in his diary.
questions.explanation: 'Can't have + past participle' expresses logical impossibility about a past action.

questions.text: The director is known for ___ attention to detail.
questions.explanation: After a preposition ('for'), a gerund (-ing form) is required.

questions.text: She congratulated him ___ passing his driving test.
questions.explanation: 'Congratulate someone on something' is the correct prepositional pattern.

questions.text: By the time we arrived, the film ___ for twenty minutes.
questions.explanation: Past perfect continuous describes an action in progress before a specific past moment.

questions.text: It's time we ___ a decision about the project.
questions.explanation: 'It's time + subject + past simple' (subjunctive) expresses that something should happen now.

questions.text: The suspect denied ___ in the area at the time.
questions.explanation: 'Deny' is followed by a gerund (-ing form), not an infinitive.

questions.text: Could you ___ me a favour and post this letter?
questions.explanation: 'Do someone a favour' is the correct collocation — not 'make' a favour.

questions.text: The company decided to lay ___ 50 workers due to budget cuts.
questions.explanation: 'Lay off' means to dismiss employees, especially temporarily, due to financial reasons.

questions.text: I wish I ___ harder when I was at school.
questions.explanation: 'Wish + past perfect' expresses regret about a past situation.

questions.text: The results were ___ better than anyone had expected.
questions.explanation: 'Far' is used with comparatives to intensify the comparison (far better, far more).

questions.text: The police are looking ___ the cause of the accident.
questions.explanation: 'Look into' means to investigate or examine a situation.

questions.text: She was made ___ wait for three hours before the interview.
questions.explanation: In the passive, 'make' takes 'to + infinitive': 'was made to wait'.

questions.text: His ___ of the situation was completely wrong.
questions.explanation: ASSESS (verb) → assessment (noun): add the suffix -ment to form a noun.

questions.text: The critics said the film was ___ long and slow.
questions.explanation: NECESSARY → unnecessary (prefix un-) → unnecessarily (suffix -ly for adverb).

questions.text: There has been a significant ___ in house prices this year.
questions.explanation: REDUCE (verb) → reduction (noun): drop -e and add -tion.

questions.text: He was ___ to take on the extra work without more pay.
questions.explanation: WILLING (adjective) → unwilling (adjective): add prefix un- to form the opposite.

questions.text: She made a ___ donation to the hospital charity.
questions.explanation: GENEROSITY (noun) → generous (adjective): the -ity ending is replaced by -ous.

questions.text: The new policy caused a lot of ___ among the staff.
questions.explanation: CERTAIN → uncertainty: add prefix un- and suffix -ty to form an abstract noun.

questions.text: The evidence against him was completely ___.
questions.explanation: REFUTE → refutable (adjective with -able) → irrefutable (prefix ir- for negation before 'r').

questions.text: His ___ of grammar rules made him a strong writer.
questions.explanation: KNOW (verb) → knowledge (noun): irregular formation involving the suffix -ledge.

questions.text: The ___ of the product was delayed by several months.
questions.explanation: INTRODUCE (verb) → introduction (noun): drop -e and add -tion.

questions.text: She gave a very ___ speech at the conference.
questions.explanation: PERSUADE (verb) → persuasive (adjective): drop -de and add -sive.
